name: Infisical
what_it_does: Open-source secrets management platform with end-to-end encryption, secret versioning, and multi-cloud sync — self-hostable or cloud-hosted.
use_cases: Self-host a secrets manager within your own infrastructure for full data sovereignty
Manage secrets across development environments with git-friendly secret push/pull workflows
Implement secret rotation for database credentials with automated rotation jobs
Sync secrets to Kubernetes using Infisical's Kubernetes operator for pod secret injection
Enforce secret access policies per service with fine-grained RBAC and audit logging
not_for: Teams needing a fully managed enterprise secrets solution without self-hosting burden
Simple CLI-based secret injection where Doppler or AWS SSM is already in place
Organizations requiring FIPS 140-2 certified HSM-backed secret storage
best_when: Best for security-conscious teams who need Vault-like capabilities but want open-source with self-hosting option.
avoid_when: Avoid when operational overhead of self-hosting and maintaining the platform outweighs the data sovereignty benefit.

auth.notes: Machine identity tokens for service-to-service auth. Universal Auth (client ID/secret), AWS Auth, Kubernetes Auth, and GCP Auth for dynamic identity federation.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Self-hosted: unlimited. Cloud free tier: 5 members, unlimited secrets
pricing.notes: MIT licensed (self-hosted). Infisical Cloud is the managed SaaS option.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted: no limits. Cloud: rate-limited per plan

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API errors follow consistent JSON format with message and statusCode fields.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Secret upserts are idempotent — creating an existing secret updates it.
known_agent_gotchas: Machine Identity tokens have TTLs — agents must refresh tokens before expiry to avoid 401 errors mid-workflow
End-to-end encryption (E2EE) mode encrypts secrets client-side — self-hosted E2EE requires additional key management
Secret paths (e.g. /backend/database) are hierarchical — agents must specify correct environment AND path for secret retrieval
Kubernetes operator syncs secrets on a polling interval (default 1 min) — secret updates don't propagate instantly to pods
Self-hosted deployments require PostgreSQL and Redis — resource requirements scale with secret volume and team size

security_score_components.security_notes: End-to-end encryption available. SOC2 certified. Multiple auth methods including cloud-native identity federation (AWS/GCP/K8s).
